title: OSI Mapping Schema
description: Schema for defining bi-directional field mappings between data sources and OSI semantic models.

Mapping.description: A single source-to-target mapping for one table/dataset pair

id.description: Source field(s) that uniquely identify a row in this source. Required when a resolution document targets the same dataset.

source_path.description: Dot-delimited path to an array field in the source object. When present, this mapping operates on each item in that array rather than on the source object itself. Use dot notation for deep nesting (e.g. 'lines.sub_items').

parent_fields.description: Pulls ancestor-level fields into scope under explicit aliases. Keys are alias names usable in expressions; values specify which ancestor field to import. Only meaningful when source_path is set.

filter_forward.description: SQL WHERE condition — only source rows matching this condition are mapped forward to the target. Used for routing one source to multiple targets.

filter_reverse.description: SQL WHERE condition — only target rows matching this condition are mapped reverse to this source. Used for selective reverse propagation.

computed_forward.description: Named computed expressions derived from source fields, available as aliases in expression_forward and filter_forward within this mapping.

computed_reverse.description: Named computed expressions derived from target fields, available as aliases in expression_reverse and filter_reverse within this mapping.

default_timestamp_field.description: Fallback source timestamp field for LAST_MODIFIED resolution. Used when a FieldMapping omits timestamp_field.

embedded.description: When true, this mapping extracts a sub-entity from the same source row as a parent mapping. The embedded entity shares the parent's source identity and has no independent existence.

field_mappings.description: Field-level mappings from source to target

ModelRef.description: Reference to a data model. Can be an OSI semantic model (semantic_model + dataset) or an external schema (schema_file).

semantic_model.description: Name of an OSI semantic model

dataset.description: Name of the dataset within the semantic model

model_file.description: Path to the OSI semantic model YAML file

schema_file.description: Path to an external schema file (JSON Schema, OpenAPI, Avro, Protobuf, etc.)

schema_path.description: JSON Pointer (RFC 6901) into the schema_file identifying the specific schema to map

FieldMapping.description: Maps a single field between source and target models. At least one of expression_forward or expression_reverse must be present; omitting both means a bidirectional copy.

target_field.description: Name of the field in the target model.

source_field.description: Name of the field in the source model. Defaults to the same value as target_field when omitted.

expression_forward.description: Expression that transforms source into the target field. When omitted, source_field is copied directly.

expression_reverse.description: Expression that transforms the target field back into the source. When omitted, target_field is copied directly.

priority.description: COALESCE ordering — lower number wins. Only meaningful when the target field's resolution strategy is COALESCE.

timestamp_field.description: Source field driving LAST_MODIFIED resolution. Overrides the mapping-level default_timestamp_field.

required.description: When true, the entire row is excluded from reverse output if this field's resolved value is null. Enables insert/delete propagation patterns.

description.description: Human-readable description of the mapping logic.

ParentFieldRef.description: Reference to an ancestor-level field, imported into scope under an alias. Only meaningful inside a mapping with source_path.

path.description: Dot-delimited path to the ancestor scope. Empty string means the root source object (parent of source_path). For deep nesting, use an intermediate path (e.g. 'lines' when source_path is 'lines.sub_items').

field.description: Field name within the ancestor scope

dialect.description: SQL or expression language dialect identifier (e.g. ANSI_SQL, SNOWFLAKE)
